verdict.answer_first: B leads with the fix: "Add it to `.gitignore`, then untrack"; A starts with context before the command.
verdict.decisiveness: A is cleaner: one path plus a separate history caveat; B adds "git filter-repo or BFG" and an extra collaborator claim.
verdict.fabrication: B has an unsafe unsupported claim: "their local `node_modules` files stay put"; tracked deletions can remove those files on pull. A: none.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it includes `git add .gitignore`; A omits staging `.gitignore` before commit.
